frames.name: Presidential Agency Frame
frames.description: Presents Trump as the decisive actor who initiated strikes to prevent Iranian aggression, emphasizing his strategic leadership
frames.emphasis: Trump's claim that he 'forced Israel's hand' rather than being pressured by Israel, positioning him as proactive rather than reactive

frames.name: Administrative Contradiction Frame
frames.description: Highlights inconsistencies between Trump's statements and those of his Secretary of State Marco Rubio
frames.emphasis: The discrepancy between Trump saying he forced Israel's hand versus Rubio's earlier statement that Israel's plans prompted US action

frames.name: Preemptive Defense Frame
frames.description: Frames the strikes as necessary defensive action based on intelligence about imminent Iranian attacks
frames.emphasis: Trump's assertion that Iran 'was going to attack first' and negotiations indicated imminent threat

frames.name: Military Success Frame
frames.description: Emphasizes the effectiveness of strikes and Iran's weakened position
frames.emphasis: Claims that 'virtually everything they have has been knocked out' and Iran's reduced capabilities

frames.name: International Relations Strain Frame
frames.description: Covers diplomatic tensions with allies over support for the strikes
frames.emphasis: Trump's criticism of Spain and UK for refusing cooperation, highlighting alliance fractures

outlierPerspectives: Iran's targeting of previously neutral countries as evidence of regime's 'evil' nature (only covered in detail by YouTube sources)
Specific casualty claims of 35,000 Iranian protesters killed (only mentioned in ABC News YouTube coverage)
Details about Iranian officials seeking immunity and potentially surrendering (unique to ABC News YouTube coverage)
missingContext: Independent verification of casualty figures and military damage claims
Details about the specific negotiations Trump referenced with Iran
Timeline and specifics of the intelligence that suggested imminent Iranian attack
Legal basis for the strikes under domestic and international law
Congressional authorization status and War Powers Act implications
Broader regional impact beyond the countries Trump mentioned Iran was striking
